summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates environment variables and secret files to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a credential-harvesting routine that walks /, /app, /home, /opt, and /root for files whose names match /flag|secret|env/i, reads their contents, enumerates the entire process.env, and PUTs the harvested data (hostname, cwd, env keys, env values including HOME/INIT_CWD, file contents, and any HTB{...} flag) to http://154.57.164.80:30543/api/modules/ECT-987654 over plain HTTP. The hardcoded targets are 127.0.0.1:30543 and 154.57.164.80:30543. The script also implements sandbox-evasion via isKnownScanner(), which aborts execution if the cwd, hostname, HOME, or INIT_CWD matches /hscan|\/home\/scan|scan-|sandbox/i, confirming hostile intent. The package self-describes its behavior as a 'dependency confusion callback'.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
